Package yams.mechanisms.positional

package yams.mechanisms.positional
Position-controlled mechanism implementations for the YAMS library.

This package contains concrete mechanism classes that track and control a position setpoint (angle, height, or displacement) using closed-loop feedback. All classes extend SmartPositionalMechanism, which provides the shared closed-loop position logic, soft-limit enforcement, simulation wiring, and WPILib command/trigger integration.

Available Mechanisms

  • Arm — single-jointed arm rotating about a fixed pivot, configured with ArmConfig
  • Elevator — linear-travel elevator stage, configured with ElevatorConfig
  • Pivot — generic pivot that may rotate continuously or within bounded limits, configured with PivotConfig
  • DifferentialMechanism — mechanism driven by two motors in a differential arrangement (e.g. differential wrist), configured with DifferentialMechanismConfig
  • DoubleJointedArm — two-segment arm whose second joint is kinematically dependent on the first, using two underlying Arm instances

Setpoint Commands

Setpoint methods return WPILib Command objects rather than setting the target synchronously. This means they compose naturally with other commands and can be cancelled, interrupted, or sequenced using the standard WPILib command scheduler: 


 // Move the arm to 45 degrees and then print a message
 arm.setAngle(Rotation2d.fromDegrees(45))
    .andThen(Commands.print("Arm at target"));

 // Move the elevator to 0.8 m, waiting until it arrives
 elevator.setHeight(Meters.of(0.8));

Trigger Factory Methods

Each positional mechanism exposes factory methods that return Trigger objects. These allow condition-based command scheduling without manual polling in periodic():

  • isNear(setpoint, tolerance) — fires while the mechanism is within tolerance of setpoint
  • max() — fires while the mechanism is at or beyond its configured maximum limit
  • min() — fires while the mechanism is at or below its configured minimum limit
  • between(lower, upper) — fires while the mechanism position is within the specified range
  • lte(position) — fires while the mechanism position is less than or equal to position
  • gte(position) — fires while the mechanism position is greater than or equal to position

 // Extend intake when arm clears 30 degrees
 arm.gte(Rotation2d.fromDegrees(30))
    .onTrue(intakeSubsystem.extend());

 // Retract intake if arm drops below 30 degrees
 arm.lte(Rotation2d.fromDegrees(30))
    .onTrue(intakeSubsystem.retract());

  • Related Packages
    Package
    Description
    yams.mechanisms
    Top-level mechanism abstractions for the YAMS (Yet Another Mechanism System) library.
    yams.mechanisms.config
    Builder-style configuration classes for every mechanism in the YAMS library.
    yams.mechanisms.swerve
    Swerve drive mechanism classes.
    yams.mechanisms.velocity
    Velocity-controlled mechanism implementations for the YAMS library.
  • Classes
    Class
    Description
    Arm
    Arm mechanism.
    DifferentialMechanism
    A differential mechanism for FRC robots driven by two coordinated motors whose combined motion produces two distinct and independent outputs.
    DoubleJointedArm
    A two-segment arm where each joint has its own independent motor controller.
    Elevator
    Elevator mechanism.
    Pivot
    Pivot mechanism.
    SmartPositionalMechanism
    Abstract intermediate base class for all closed-loop positional mechanisms in YAMS.
